timeline.label: Q1 2026 net income $2.46B ($9.25 diluted EPS) versus $596M ($2.11) in Q1 2025; continued strong underwriting
FY2025 10-K: combined ratio 85.2%, underwriting income $8.54B, book value $108.45/sh, ROE 42.3%
Board authorized new $4.0B share repurchase program after completion of $1.5B program; 2025 repurchases $1.24B (6M shares)
Transformative Growth strategy: multi-channel distribution (exclusive, independent, direct) to grow personal-lines share at lower expense ratio
FY2024 combined ratio improved to 94.3% from 104.5% in 2023 as rate actions took hold
FY2023 underwriting loss year: combined ratio 104.5% amid auto/home loss inflation
National General integration continues under Allstate Protection multi-brand distribution
Protection Services expanded (roadside, protection plans, Arity telematics) beyond core P&C underwriting

industry.structure: U.S. personal auto and homeowners insurance is concentrated among a handful of national carriers (State Farm, Progressive, Allstate, GEICO) with high marketing intensity and state-by-state rate regulation.
industry.share_shift: Direct-channel insurers gained auto share in the 2010s; incumbents with multi-channel models (Allstate, Progressive) are reinvesting in pricing sophistication and expense cuts to defend share.
industry.trend: 2022-2024 loss inflation forced industry-wide rate increases; 2025-2026 focus shifts to profit retention, catastrophe reinsurance costs, and telematics-based underwriting (Arity).
